name: Pas Greenway Loop
name_original: Parque de la Naturaleza de Cabárceno por la Vía Verde de Pas – circular desde Sarón
description: This moderate 17.4-mile touring cycling route combines the Vía Verde del Pas with the unique wildlife and landscapes of Cabárceno Nature Par
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Santa María De Cayón are situated within the Pas-Miera valley in Cantabria, Spain. The region features diverse natural landscapes, including gentle valleys, forested areas, and more challenging mountain terrain. The Pisueña River contributes to the area's lush greenery, while hills like Monte Carceña offer elevated perspectives. The terrain varies from mostly paved, easy surfaces to unpaved segments with significant elevation gain. Best touring cycling routes around Santa María De Cayón The most popular touring cycling route is Pas Greenway Loop, a 17.4 miles (28.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 48 minutes to complete. This route follows a former railway line, offering a largely flat path through natural surroundings. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Reyerma Steam Locomotive – Puente Viesgo loop from Santa María de Cayón, an easy 14.8 miles (23.8 km) path. This route features the historic Reyerma Steam Locomotive and sections of greenway. Local touring cyclists also love the View from Peña Cabarga – Vía Verde del Pas loop from La Penilla, a 31.9 miles (51.4 km) trail leading through the Peña Cabarga viewpoint and parts of the Vía Verde del Pas, often completed in about 3 hours 56 minutes. Touring cycling around Santa María De Cayón is defined by lush valleys, forested hills, and former railway lines converted into greenways. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy, family-friendly paths to challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Santa María De Cayón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from around 20 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Santa María De Cayón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Santa María De Cayón?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes documented on komoot for the Santa María De Cayón area. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy, mostly paved paths to challenging rides with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Santa María De Cayón offers several easy touring cycling routes. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38187093" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reyerma Steam Locomotive – Puente Viesgo loop from Santa María de Cayón&lt;/a&gt;, which is 23.8 km long with minimal elevation gain. This route incorporates sections of greenway and passes by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5410560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reyerma Steam Locomotive&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Santa María De Cayón?
A: The terrain around Santa María De Cayón is quite diverse. You'll find routes that follow former railway lines, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5156745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pas Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, which are largely flat and paved. Other routes venture into forested areas and more challenging mountain terrain, with some segments potentially unpaved and involving significant elevation changes, such as the route to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2595732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Peña Cabarga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Santa María De Cayón are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012451006/pas-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pas Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38176930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de La Braguía – Church of Vega de Pas loop from Santa María de Cayón&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a more challenging experience.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see along the cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can discover a variety of interesting sights. You might pass by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5410560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reyerma Steam Locomotive&lt;/a&gt;, the symbolic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrim Monument&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2595732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Peña Cabarga&lt;/a&gt;. For natural beauty, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5156745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pas Greenway&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4146541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Churrón de Borleña Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Santa María De Cayón?
A: The touring cycling routes around Santa María De Cayón are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from nearly 20 reviews. Over 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the diverse landscapes, from lush valleys to scenic greenways, and the variety of routes suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38176930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de La Braguía – Church of Vega de Pas loop from Santa María de Cayón&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain (nearly 1,900m) over 91.2 km. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20161126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Pozos de Noja – Pozos de Noja loop from Santa María de Cayón&lt;/a&gt;, featuring almost 1,000m of ascent.

Q: Can I find routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Yes, several routes provide excellent viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38210275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Peña Cabarga – Vía Verde del Pas loop from La Penilla&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights the stunning vistas from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2595732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Cabarga&lt;/a&gt;. Other elevated points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/810123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Blancas Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3397254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Llen and Peña Cabarga Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible and offer panoramic views of the region.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Santa María De Cayón?
A: Santa María De Cayón, located in Cantabria, Spain, generally offers pleasant conditions for touring cycling from spring through autumn. The lush greenery is particularly vibrant in spring, while summer provides warmer temperatures. Autumn brings beautiful foliage. It's advisable to check local weather forecasts, as the region can experience varied conditions.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through or near the Vía Verde del Pas?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5156745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Pas&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature in the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012451006/pas-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pas Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 28 km route that directly utilizes this former railway line. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38210275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Peña Cabarga – Vía Verde del Pas loop from La Penilla&lt;/a&gt;, also incorporates sections of the Vía Verde del Pas, combining it with more challenging terrain.
tips: A high-rise stone structure symbolizing an imaginary church halfway between the Romanesque and Gothic styles. Under the representation of a portico in which different trades in the area are represented, there is an altar. There is also a sculpture of a pilgrim that symbolizes the search for truth and four reliefs that represent Santiago, Santo Toribio, San Pedro and Jesus. It is designed by José Antonio Barquín Ruiz in 1958 as a tribute to the pilgrims on the Camino de Santiago del Norte.

This green path runs along the old railway line that linked Astillero and Ontaneda with the Cantabrian coast. In total, it is a journey of about 34 km between green grasslands, deciduous forests and small livestock farms.

If you are a lover of animals and nature you have to visit this place. In it, you can see numerous animals, from elephants, bears, gorillas and wolves to numerous farm animals, a reptile house, an exhibition of birds of prey and sea lions. The display of birds of prey is impressive. They explain to you how each one acts when looking for food.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route offers varied terrain. The Pas Greenway (Astillero) section is largely flat and well-paved, following an old railway line. However, cycling within Cabárceno Nature Park involves a 20-kilometer network of paved roads with at least two substantial uphill segments, reaching elevations up to 276 meters. Be prepared for some elevation changes within the park.

faqs.question: Is this circular route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While the Vía Verde del Pas itself is generally easy and suitable for beginners, this specific circular route from Sarón is rated as moderate. The sections within Cabárceno Nature Park include significant uphill climbs, requiring good fitness. Beginners might find these sections challenging, but e-bike tours are available within Cabárceno for assisted rides.

faqs.question: What unique sights and wildlife can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route uniquely combines picturesque natural scenery with an extraordinary wildlife experience. Along the Vía Verde del Pas, you'll see green Cantabrian landscapes, clear rivers, and charming villages. The highlight is Cabárceno Nature Park, a former mining operation now home to nearly 120 animal species from various continents, including elephants, bears, giraffes, zebras, and tigers, living in semi-freedom. You'll also pass striking rock formations and viewpoints like 'La trinchera' overlooking Lago Sexta.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this route from Sarón?
faqs.answer: Sarón serves as the starting and ending point for this circular route. While specific parking facilities for the Vía Verde del Pas near Sarón are not detailed, you can typically find parking within the town or at designated access points for the greenway. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Cycling on the Vía Verde del Pas does not require permits or fees. However, Cabárceno Nature Park is a paid attraction. You will need to purchase an entrance ticket to cycle through the park and observe the animals. Check the park's official website for current admission prices and operating hours.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) when the weather is mild and pleasant. Summer can be warm, especially in Cabárceno Nature Park, and winter can bring cooler temperatures and potentially rain. Visiting outside of peak tourist season might also offer a more tranquil experience.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The Vía Verde del Pas is generally dog-friendly, allowing leashed dogs. However, Cabárceno Nature Park has specific rules regarding pets due to the presence of wild animals. Typically, dogs are not allowed inside the animal enclosures or certain areas of the park. It's best to confirm the park's current pet policy directly before planning your visit with a dog.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find opportunities for refreshments. The Vía Verde del Pas passes through charming villages in Cantabria where you can find local cuisine, cafes, and restaurants. Within Cabárceno Nature Park, there are also facilities offering food and drinks for visitors.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates significant sections of the Vía Verde del Pas. It also passes through parts of the Camino Natural de Villaescusa and the Senda del Litoral, offering connections to other regional trails.

faqs.question: Can I rent a bike in Sarón or near the Vía Verde del Pas?
faqs.answer: While specific rental shops in Sarón are not detailed, bike rental services are often available in towns along popular greenways like the Vía Verde del Pas. It's recommended to search for local bike rental shops in Sarón or nearby larger towns in advance of your trip.

faqs.question: Is there public transport available to reach Sarón for this route?
faqs.answer: Sarón is a well-connected town in Cantabria. Public transport options, primarily bus services, are available to reach Sarón from larger cities in the region. Check local bus schedules for routes connecting to Sarón.
